headlines.title: Flesh-eating screwworm returns to U.S. after 60 years, threatening cattle herd
headlines.description: A flesh-eating parasite that had been kept out of U.S. livestock for decades has been detected in Texas, threatening the nation’s cattle industry and food supply at a time when prices are already high.

headlines.title: SpaceX aims to make its stock market debut at $1.77 trillion value
headlines.description: Elon Musk’s space company said in a securities filing that it hopes to sell over 555 million shares at its debut for $135 apiece.

headlines.title: FAA probes close call between JetBlue plane and aircraft near Fort Lauderdale Airport
headlines.description: The JetBlue plane received an onboard alert warning about another nearby aircraft that was “not in communications with air traffic control,” according to the FAA.

headlines.title: CBS News’ Bari Weiss addresses ‘unfortunate’ firing of Scott Pelley
headlines.description: CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss, speaking to network employees Wednesday about the firing of “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley, said newsroom leaders could not “find a way back” with the veteran journalist — a comment he flatly rejected in a statement of his own

headlines.title: June 2026 Freebies: Bowling for Kids, Gym Memberships, Donuts
headlines.description: From free bowling to complimentary gym memberships, NBC’s chief consumer investigative correspondent Vicky Nguyen joins TODAY with a roundup of ways to cash in on freebies for the month of June.

headlines.title: Pickle, Too Good to Go: Buzzy Apps to Help Save Money
headlines.description: TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones highlight two of the internet’s most buzzed about apps: Pickle, which allows users to rent things from others’ closets, and Too Good to Go, which can help people save money on food and cut down on waste.

headlines.title: Should You Trust AI With Your Finances? What You Need to Know
headlines.description: Artificial intelligence is everywhere but should you trust it with your personal finances? Certified financial planner Ted Jenkin joins TODAY to break down how AI can help you manage your money and when you should use it with caution. He recommends using artificial intelligence to help with budgeting and organization but warns against oversharing personal information. “Use general concepts, general ideas. Always verify information. You shouldn't blindly trust all the financial advice that you

headlines.title: Kevin O'Leary addresses 'misinformation' on Utah data center project
headlines.description: NBC News' Tom Llamas talks to Kevin O'Leary on his plans for a data center in Utah, saying there is a lot of "misinformation" and they will not touch the Great Salt Lake.

headlines.title: How shoppers who pay in cash are subsidizing Americans’ credit card reward points
headlines.description: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

headlines.title: How grocery prices have changed since Trump took office
headlines.description: Learn where grocery prices stand during the Trump administration. See how inflation is affecting the cost of eggs, bread, chicken, ground beef and more.

headlines.title: Jozy Altidore, now a broadcaster, is bullish on the U.S. making a deep World Cup run
headlines.description: The former striker will serve as a World Cup commentator for Telemundo, the tournament’s official Spanish-language network.

headlines.title: Student who called out CBS at News Emmys says Scott Pelley did the ‘right thing’
headlines.description: Moments before Santiago Campos took the stage at the News & Documentary Emmy Awards in New York City to accept a CBS News-funded scholarship in front of some of the most powerful people in TV news, veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley took the time to offer the high school senior some advice.
